---
title: "Base Image"
description: "Choose a base image or existing template when building Go templates."
---

# Base Image

Every Go template starts from either a Docker image or another E2B template.

In the Go SDK, `e2b.Template(nil)` already starts from the default E2B base image, `e2bdev/base`.

## Create a template

`TemplateOptions` controls how local files are resolved while you build the template definition.

- `FileContextPath` is the base directory for local file inputs such as `Copy(...)` and `FromDockerfile("Dockerfile")`
- `FileIgnorePatterns` adds extra ignore patterns when local files are hashed or uploaded

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	template := e2b.Template(&e2b.TemplateOptions{
		FileContextPath:    ".",
		FileIgnorePatterns: []string{".git", "node_modules"},
	})

	_ = template
}
```

## Use a predefined base image

The root package exposes convenience helpers for the common image families.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	defaultBase := e2b.Template(nil)
	explicitBase := e2b.Template(nil).FromBaseImage()
	ubuntu := e2b.Template(nil).FromUbuntuImage("22.04")
	debian := e2b.Template(nil).FromDebianImage("stable-slim")
	python := e2b.Template(nil).FromPythonImage("3.13")
	node := e2b.Template(nil).FromNodeImage("lts")
	bun := e2b.Template(nil).FromBunImage("1.3")

	_, _, _, _, _, _, _ = defaultBase, explicitBase, ubuntu, debian, python, node, bun
}
```

Default helper versions in the current Go SDK:

- `Template(nil)` -> `e2bdev/base`
- `FromBaseImage()` -> `e2bdev/base`
- `FromDebianImage()` -> `debian:stable`
- `FromUbuntuImage()` -> `ubuntu:latest`
- `FromPythonImage()` -> `python:3`
- `FromNodeImage()` -> `node:lts`
- `FromBunImage()` -> `oven/bun:latest`

## Use a custom image or existing template

Use `FromImage(...)` for arbitrary Docker images, or `FromTemplate(...)` to build on top of an existing E2B template.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	fromImage := e2b.Template(nil).FromImage("custom-image:latest")

	fromPrivate := e2b.Template(nil).FromImage("ghcr.io/acme/private:latest", &e2b.RegistryCredentials{
		Username: "user",
		Password: "token",
	})

	fromAWS := e2b.Template(nil).FromAWSRegistry(
		"123456789.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/app:latest",
		&e2b.AWSRegistryCredentials{
			AccessKeyID:     "AKIA...",
			SecretAccessKey: "...",
			Region:          "us-west-2",
		},
	)

	fromGCP := e2b.Template(&e2b.TemplateOptions{FileContextPath: "."}).FromGCPRegistry(
		"gcr.io/myproject/app:latest",
		&e2b.GCPRegistryCredentials{ServiceAccountJSON: "service-account.json"},
	)

	fromTemplate := e2b.Template(nil).FromTemplate("my-template")
	fromNamespacedTemplate := e2b.Template(nil).FromTemplate("acme/other-template")

	_, _, _, _, _, _ = fromImage, fromPrivate, fromAWS, fromGCP, fromTemplate, fromNamespacedTemplate
}
```

For registry authentication details, see [Private Registries](/docs/template/private-registries).

## Parse an existing Dockerfile

`FromDockerfile(...)` accepts either:

- raw Dockerfile content
- a file path such as `"Dockerfile"`, resolved from `TemplateOptions.FileContextPath`

If `FileContextPath` is omitted, relative Dockerfile paths are resolved from the caller directory.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	inline := e2b.Template(nil).FromDockerfile(`FROM ubuntu:22.04
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV PORT=3000
USER appuser`)

	fromPath := e2b.Template(&e2b.TemplateOptions{
		FileContextPath: ".",
	}).FromDockerfile("Dockerfile")

	inline.SetStartCmd("npm start", e2b.WaitForTimeout(5_000))

	_ = inline
	_ = fromPath
}
```

Current parser behavior in the Go SDK:

- `FROM` selects the base image
- `RUN` becomes `RunCmd(...)`
- `COPY` and `ADD` become `Copy(...)`
- `WORKDIR` becomes `SetWorkdir(...)`
- `USER` becomes `SetUser(...)`
- `ENV` and `ARG` become `SetEnvs(...)`
- `CMD` and `ENTRYPOINT` become `SetStartCmd(...)` with `WaitForTimeout(20_000)` as the ready command
- `EXPOSE` and `VOLUME` are ignored
- multi-stage Dockerfiles are rejected

If the Dockerfile does not set a final `USER` or `WORKDIR`, the current Go parser finishes with E2B defaults: `user` and `/home/user`. If the Dockerfile already sets them, those custom values are preserved.

For the full template builder surface, see the [Go SDK Template reference](/docs/sdk-reference/go-sdk/template).
